2604.11791.title: A Mechanistic Analysis of Looped Reasoning Language Models
2604.11791.paper_summary: Reasoning has become a central capability in large language models. Recent research has shown that reasoning performance can be improved by looping an LLM's layers in the latent dimension, resulting in looped reasoning language models. Despite promising results, few works have investigated how their internal dynamics differ from those of standard feedforward models. In this paper, we conduct a mechanistic analysis of the latent states in looped language models, focusing in particular on how the stages of inference observed in feedforward models compare to those observed in looped ones. To this end, we analyze cyclic recurrence and show that for many of the studied models each layer in the cycle converges to a distinct fixed point; consequently, the recurrent block follows a consistent cyclic trajectory in the latent space. We provide evidence that as these fixed points are reached, attention-head behavior stabilizes, leading to constant behavior across recurrences. Empirically, we discover that recurrent blocks learn stages of inference that closely mirror those of feedforward models, repeating these stages in depth with each iteration. We study how recurrent block size, input injection, and normalization influence the emergence and stability of these cyclic fixed points. We believe these findings help translate mechanistic insights into practical guidance for architectural design.
2604.11791.paper_summary_zh: 推理已成為大型語言模型的核心能力。最近的研究顯示，通過在潛在維度中循環LLM的層，可以改善推理性能，從而產生循環推理語言模型。儘管結果令人鼓舞，但很少有研究探討它們的內部動態與標準前饋模型有何不同。在本文中，我們對循環語言模型中的潛在狀態進行機制分析，特別關注在前饋模型中觀察到的推理階段與在循環模型中觀察到的推理階段之間的比較。為此，我們分析了循環重複，並顯示在許多研究的模型中，循環中的每一層都收斂到一個不同的固定點；因此，循環區塊在潛在空間中遵循一致的循環軌跡。我們提供證據表明，當這些固定點被達到時，注意力頭的行為穩定，導致在重複過程中行為保持不變。實證上，我們發現循環區塊學習的推理階段與前饋模型的階段非常相似，並在每次迭代中深入重複這些階段。我們研究了循環區塊大小、輸入注入和正規化如何影響這些循環固定點的出現和穩定性。我們相信這些發現有助於將機制洞察轉化為架構設計的實用指導。
2604.11791.author: Hugh Blayney et.al.
2604.11791.authors: Hugh Blayney, Álvaro Arroyo, Johan Obando-Ceron, Pablo Samuel Castro, Aaron Courville, Michael M. Bronstein, Xiaowen Dong
